Description: Budget hotel at I-40 Exit 128 (Fairgrove Church Rd) in Conover. Booking listings (Expedia) note free self parking plus RV, bus and truck parking; also listed as a trucker-usable hotel on Trucker Path.

24-hour Pilot Travel Center at I-40 Exit 81 (Sugar Hill Rd), Marion. 6 diesel fuel lanes with DEF, 40 truck parking spots (9 reserved/paid), 4 showers, public laundry, CAT scale, Subway and Mama DeLuca's Pizza, ATM and convenience store.

Small independent truck stop at I-40 Exit 90 (Nebo/Lake James). Diesel (2 lanes), about 15 truck parking spaces with overnight parking, showers, laundry, trucker lounge, convenience store and adjacent restaurant; also a U-Haul rental point. Reviews confirm it active through 2025-2026.

Budget hotel near I-40 Exit 85 (US 221), Marion. Explicitly advertises free RV, bus and truck parking in its lot, plus free light breakfast and free Wi-Fi.

Business Name: McDowell County Rest Area I-40 Eastbound (MM 82)
Description: NCDOT rest area on I-40 eastbound at mile marker 82, McDowell County (between exits 81 and 85). Listed in the official NCDOT rest area locations document, but no civic street address is published, so held per weigh-station/rest-area rule. Recent traveler reviews (through June 2026) indicate it is open.

Business Name: Stuckey's / Dairy Queen / Exxon of Old Fort
Description: Historic Stuckey's with co-located Dairy Queen and Exxon fuel (regular through diesel) at I-40 Exit 75 (Parker Padgett Rd), Old Fort; Allstays lists truck parking. The building burned in a fire on Feb. 28 (McDowell News); owner said he hoped to reopen within months, but no source confirms a completed reopening, so current operation cannot be verified. Old Fort area also saw heavy Hurricane Helene damage (Sept 2024).
